steps.title: Join the two clauses
steps.content: The sentence has two independent clauses. The first says the interpretations are speculative, and though qualifies that statement. The second explains why: the gray color may come from radiation, solar wind, or collisions. Because both clauses could stand as sentences, comma-only punctuation would be too weak in this sentence.

steps.title: Place the clause boundary
steps.content: Choice A lacks a boundary before the second independent clause. Choice B attaches though to the first clause with a comma and uses a semicolon before the next clause. Choice C misplaces though. Choice D creates a comma splice. The answer is B.
